reviews.review_description: By removing PopulationOne from Steam for new players while still allowing existing owners to continue playing, you’ve effectively locked in the top 1% and eliminated any real opportunity for others to challenge them on equal footing. This doesn’t solve the cheating problem, it just preserves the advantage for those already on the platform. Either remove Steam access entirely to create a level playing field, or restore access for everyone. As it stands, cheating wasn’t stopped; the imbalance was just made permanent.

reviews.review_title: GAME DEVELOPERS are KILLING the GAME!!!
reviews.review_description:
This is my favorite VR game. I’ve been playing it for 4 years. But it’s getting more and more problems, and I’ve run out of patience!

1. The game forces good players to always wait 350 seconds!
It’s not right that the game punishes the people who love it and have been playing it for a long time.
Every time I join classic mode, I wait 6 minutes just because I’ve been playing for a long time and I'm good at it. This happens constantly, while dozens of my friends play in full lobbies!
I wait and end up with nothing but bots.
I know many people who quit the game because of this.
And now I’m starting to feel the same way!
If I want to play against bots, there's a separate mode for that — and without the wait!
Classic is broken! Fix it or bring back the Evolving map.
Evolving was much better — no bots, no long waiting, and a more interesting map.

2. Constant crashes, bugs, and freezes.
The game is in a terrible technical state.
Not a day goes by without the game glitching, freezing, or losing sound.
The developers keep changing things, but never fix the major issues!
The state of the game keeps getting worse every year I play.

3. Skins that don’t match hitboxes.
More and more often, devs add ridiculous skins with massive proportions: ice cream, cookies, hot dogs… and now pizza, which is completely unacceptable!
Tell me — how am I supposed to land a headshot on a pizza skin when the head is enormous, but bullets go right through it? To deal damage, I have to hit a small invisible point somewhere inside that oversized mess!

There are many other small issues, but if the developers don’t fix these critical ones soon, the game will simply die — and I really don’t want that to happen.

I love this game, but I have to give it one star in the hope that someone will hear me.

I really love this game and find the community generally pretty good. I do feel it is a shame that the developers are projecting their own value sets onto their paying audience. Yes, I get that the game is fundamentally social in nature, but sometimes you don’t want to communicate with 2 or 5 randoms and just want the flexibility to choose a solo mode. This has been implemented before, then always removed. So why not listen to your customers and just add a permanent solo mode? People who love the social side of the game can continue to enjoy the co-op modes, but, people like me, who sometimes prefer to be lone wolves and have really debilitating phases of social anxiety, can enjoy the game in their own way. Being social isn’t always about being ‘social.’ It can mean recognising the needs of everyone in your community and responding to those needs, rather than applying your own sensibilities and perceived norms.

The game is really fun but I have an idea for joining friends in games like let’s say there in hangout map or mixed playlist or in training park I think it will be cool if you clicked on the name and it tells you what there in and you are able to join them in the same server but it’s only an idea pls add this.

Population One is the best game for meeting interesting people, learning how to work together, and ultimately just have a fun time. Being a quest user and often just too opinionated..I'm learning where I'm wrong as to what a pc population one steam players can do. The pc steam cheater has really made me to quick to think someone is cheating...most of the time it's just simple that they are a better player. I apologize!

To be honest, this game is the only reason I use my meta quest at all. A great example of what's possible with our current technology. HOWEVER, since playing over the last 8 months, a few major flaws have become quite prominent. Seeing to these problems would make the game a lot more enjoyable, rather than feeling as if I've cheated myself with the thrill of VR itself. Here are some points that I think are important:

A) BOTS
I understand that the player base is thin, and you want to put us right into the game with as little loading time as possible. But honestly, I'd rather wait an extra minute or two to fill the lobby with real players. Unless you seriously upgrade the bot AI, you may as well just not have them in the game at all as they feel more like a chore than a target when in-game.

B) Stuck on walls!
I'm forever getting stuck on walls and parts of the solid environment. Really annoying and game breaking when trying to get away from or chase a player. 

C) Another map?
I'm just talking about battle royale here again. There is just one map for the standard squads, which I'm kind of sick of now. Please add another map for us to use and vote for in battle royale. 

D) Bigger player lobby/ more time!
The games just aren't long enough. Easily goes from 16 players to 8 in just a few minutes. A bigger map and longer lobby waiting times could improve this maybe? A standard game lasts less than 10 minutes usually, really not that great and very tedious between matches.

End. 

These changes could make this game incredible. It's the ideal battle royal/ sandbox game; just needs a little bit more love put into it! I'd happily pay for the game if it had all these features

reviews.review_title: I can't log into the game for 2 days
reviews.review_description:
I'm giving it 1 star because the game just stopped starting. It freezes on the last screensaver and nothing happens after that.

What I tried:

I reinstalled the game 2 times - it didn't help.

I rebooted the helmet - to no avail.

Cleared the cache - no effect.

Judging by the reviews, this problem is widespread and has been going on for 2 weeks, but the developers are not fixing anything. I can't log into the game for 2 days. Until you fix it, the rating will remain the same. Fix it!”

reviews.review_title: By far my favorite vr game
reviews.review_description: I only have one issue. The hackers. I encounter a hacker every game that I play. I watched someone shoot me while I was behind them. I have been shot with a pistol through a wall from across the map (from armory to whatever that area by summit is called) for 138 damage. I was at 100hp and 38sp. I was not in front of a window. My teammates that game said they saw it too. Love the game, no problem with it, could use the regular game mode on the other map though. Would like solo mode, but overall great game. One more thing, could we have age filters for who your team is? I had a 6 year old beg me to send him a friend request.

reviews.review_title: Great game, training lobby needs more
reviews.review_description:
I've only played a few matches, but the game became easy to understand once I got into actual gameplay. The training lobby could use some improvements, though.

Important mechanics like gliding and reviving teammates aren't explained, at all. Gold Rings platform could tell players to spread their arms to glide and include a simple revive/resuscitation exercise with a dummy bot. A short section on using the map and ping system to find teammates would also help new players. Reduce the obstacle course to make room for this. 

The game is fun overall, but the tutorial could do a better job preparing players for real matches.

It worked great and I enjoyed the game. But out of nowhere the game stopped loading it. It just says, "logging on, please wait" but never actually starts. I tried uninstall and reinstall, switching networks, rebooting my headset, and force stopping and starting the app. Nothing has worked. But the game is a lot of fun.. just doesn't seem to work anymore. Hopefully they get it fixed. I'm on quest 3, fully up to date, as is the game. If it loads for you, you'll have a good time just know it might suddenly stop working. Devs, please fix this, it's a great game 🙏

reviews.review_title: Long time owner, just playing now :(
reviews.review_description:
​I honestly can't believe I let Population: ONE sit in my library for years untouched. 

As someone with both a Quest and a PCVR setup, I was always distracted by other titles. I finally decided to try it out while traveling on holiday, and wow... I’ve been seriously missing out.

​The game is just flat-out fun. The current community is generally great, and while there are definitely bots mixed into games, the core PvP experience is solid. 

The real magic happens when you actually work with your squad as a team. If you haven’t tried it yet—or if it’s just collecting digital dust like it was for me—grab some friends and jump in.

I know Meta has started to really abandon this game, but there is a massive fan base for this game that would easily be revived if they could figure out how to offer us different things to buy other than skins. We will put money into it, if you figure out how to format the game a little differently. Create lobbies where special abilities can be used--flying, instant reloading, and more in not thinking of. There are many people on the Meta Quests who almost exclusively play this game. 

Offer us a different "world"/layout to play in, and make it so people can actually be stealthy and hide in, under, and behind things like in fortnight. I'm not a developer, so I don't know the perfect solutions, but I know I and many others like me are ready and willing to invest in this game if you'll give us something other than skins to purchase.

I love this game and it is like Fortnite but with call of duty graphics. The one thing I don’t really like is the matchmaking, like sometimes it puts me in a squad of two against a bunch of squads of 3. I still win those games becuz I’m just good but for other people it makes it unfair.

It’s sad to see this game going downhill so fast. So many bugs and hackers that devs never seem to do anything about. Every match has a bug whether there’s no game audio but voice chat works or the game freezes and crashes. The rocks render terribly so you can’t shoot players or the enemy can see you through them. Not to mention it’s pretty much the same map the game has had since launch. New content besides skins is very very very rarely added. So many players “mod” the game on PC to add unfair advantages to their game. No matter how many times you or other players report the cheaters you’ll see them in matches day after day. The recently implemented 6 minute lobby is hurting the game more than helping. You spend 6 minutes waiting just to get more full games but it’s always the same people over and over and over and over. Seems like the player rotation was way better when lobbies were 3-4 minutes. I’d rather play a game with 3-4 squads than wait 6 minutes to play the same 6 squads I played the last 5 matches. I’ve had this game since it had to be purchased years ago and do enjoy some of the community aspects but it’s almost not even worth it anymore.

I took a 2 month break from the game and came back once I heard they banned the hackers. Nope. Got head shot sniped by a guy running from out of the Tower while I was elevated hundreds of meters away. There was no way he could have known I was camping there yet he took me out. I also dumped a full 3-star UMG clip into a guy to have him shoot me twice and kill me when I had full shield. I then ghosted him to watch him find my teammates hiding in a barn. How did he know they were hiding in the upper corners? He knew exactly where they were. Please detect and block the hackers. This game is fun when folks play straight up than to repeatedly get slaughtered by these hacks.

reviews.review_description:
I just started the game and already hate it! Here's why: 
There's nothing but no lifers one shotting you from across the map. 
They climb to the tallest tower and just camp and some how hit me like twice and I'm dead, even tho I have full health and shield.

Then there's player created maps which also blow, I tried the one that's like nuketown from COD and all the other team does is sit on the roof and camp or sit in ur spawn and camp, so it's spawn die, spawn die, repeate....GARBAGE. Just go play COD 🤦‍♀️

reviews.review_title: Listen. Listen to what we are asking.
reviews.review_description:
Having only been around a year or two, I’ve gotten very attached to this game but it needs some work and it needs some true artistic creation. 

-Create a conversion system for seeker silver to bureau gold because you have not updated the social store.
-Add more to social store.
-Other ways to earn Bureau Gold.
-Better skin designs instead of rehashing old and boring skins. Go back and look at what people are wearing. Put those out.
-Design a new map or two.
-Work on making black boxing less of an issue.
-Listen to what we are telling you.
-Put out a contest for skin designs.
-Add more to the public sandbox creation.
New buildings. New natural items. New templates.
-Add some new weapons, I'm sure people would love to get a chance to learn new things.

Love this game but I’m honestly super disappointed right now. Even after recent updates the cheating problem still feels out of control, with players clearly getting away with it and ruining matches, which makes the ban system feel pointless. On top of that, the game starts feeling dead after about 30 minutes because the content is so limited. It’s basically just squads over and over, and the sandbox modes are boring and mostly filled with kids messing around instead of real gameplay. Another huge issue is the bot to real player ratio, which makes matches feel fake and lifeless and is a big reason the game starts feeling like a simulation instead of a real competitive shooter. After a while it turns into the same loop of drop, fight, die, repeat with nothing new or exciting happening. The movement and shooting are still fun, but they can’t carry the whole experience forever. We seriously need more actually fun game modes, ranked or objective-based options, better progression, and real anti-cheat enforcement. I’d even pay for cheater-free servers at this point. The game has so much potential, but right now it feels stale, frustrating, and way less enjoyable than it should be…
